summary: Self-declared supply-chain attack PoC executes arbitrary command on install
details: The package is an openly-labeled supply-chain attack demonstration. Its `postinstall` lifecycle hook (`node -e "try { require('./dist/postinstall.js'); } catch(e) {}"`) loads `dist/postinstall.js`, which contains a bundled IIFE that calls `child_process.exec` with a platform-specific payload on every install: `open -a Calculator` on macOS, `calc.exe` on Windows, and `gnome-calculator || xcalc` on Linux. The same payload is also embedded in `dist/index.js` (the declared `main`), so it additionally fires on `require()` of the package. While the current payload is a benign calculator launch, the package's stated purpose is to prove that arbitrary unsolicited code executes on installer machines, and the mechanism is indistinguishable from a live attack — swapping the command string in a future version yields immediate RCE on every installer. Installing or loading this package causes the installer to execute attacker-chosen shell commands without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
